job.title: Bilingual Resident Services Specialist - Sandy Springs, GA
job.description:
Cortland runs one of the larger vertically integrated multifamily platforms in the country, and their centralized contact center model means this role looks a little different from a typical on-site leasing consultant position. You're not walking apartments or collecting keys. You're the voice residents hear when something needs to get resolved, and you're handling that across multiple properties at once.

What This Role Actually Involves

As a Bilingual Resident Services Specialist based in Sandy Springs, you'll work inside Cortland's contact center, fielding inbound calls, emails, and chat messages from residents across the portfolio. The bilingual piece matters here. You'll be communicating in both English and Spanish, which means your ability to de-escalate a tense situation or explain a lease detail clearly doesn't get to stop at one language.

The volume is real. You'll manage multiple conversations simultaneously, toggle between platforms like RealPage, OneSite, and Funnel, and stay organized while priorities shift. Some of what lands in your queue will be simple. Some of it won't. Difficult residents, time-sensitive maintenance concerns, and sensitive account questions are part of the daily mix, not the exception.

  • Handle resident inquiries across phone, email, and chat with accuracy and professionalism
  • De-escalate complaints and document interactions within CRM tools
  • Navigate multiple property management platforms and adapt as systems change
  • Maintain Fair Housing compliance on every interaction, without exception
  • Work collaboratively within the contact center team toward shared service goals
  • Maintain availability for weekend and extended hours as business needs require

What Cortland Is Looking For

Two or more years of customer service experience is required, preferably in a high-volume contact center or multifamily setting. A high school diploma or equivalent is the minimum education requirement. You'll need solid proficiency in Microsoft Office and comfort with property management software platforms. Strong time management isn't a soft skill here. It's a daily operational requirement.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this kind of role is emotional steadiness. It's easy to be pleasant when the call is easy. The candidates who do well here stay calm and solution-oriented when a resident is frustrated, when systems are slow, and when the queue isn't shrinking. Bilingual fluency in Spanish is a core requirement, not a bonus.

The Bigger Picture

Contact center roles at large operators like Cortland tend to build a skill set that translates well across the industry. You'll develop fluency with enterprise-level property management platforms, a strong understanding of resident retention, and experience handling compliance-sensitive conversations at scale. Those skills carry real weight if you later move toward an on-site community manager role or a regional support function. This isn't a dead-end position. It's a clear entry point into a company that promotes from within and has the portfolio size to back that up.

job.title: Experienced Field Maintenance Technician (3+ Years Required)
job.description:
You're wrapping up a kitchen faucet repair at one property when a new work order drops on your phone: a unit across town needs a door lock replaced before a new resident moves in tomorrow. You close out the first ticket, update your notes, and head out. That rhythm of owning your schedule and your work quality is exactly what this role is built around.

What Lessen Is Looking For

Lessen operates a tech-enabled property services platform that manages maintenance across more than a million properties nationwide, completing millions of work orders every year through a network of vetted vendors and in-house technicians. The Birmingham territory is part of that broader national expansion, and they're staffing it with people who can work independently without needing hand-holding on every job.

The minimum bar here is real: three or more years of verifiable, hands-on experience in property maintenance or a related trade. Not "some experience." Not a background that's mostly one discipline. They want someone who has seen enough different problems to think through solutions rather than just escalate them. You'll own your work orders from assignment to close-out, manage job-level budgets, and maintain quality across a portfolio of residential properties.

  • At least 3 years of hands-on property maintenance or trade experience (required)
  • Ability to manage multiple work orders independently and prioritize effectively
  • Comfort with a tech platform for receiving, updating, and closing work orders
  • Solution-oriented mindset when facing unfamiliar or complex repair situations
  • Valid driver's license (company fleet vehicle is provided)

The Setup

A few things about this role worth knowing up front. There's no on-call rotation. Weekday schedule. That's a real differentiator in maintenance work, where after-hours calls are often just accepted as part of the deal. Lessen is structured differently, and for experienced techs who are tired of the 10pm emergency calls, that matters.

Pay starts between $23 and $27 per hour depending on your skills and experience. On top of that, there's a quarterly discretionary bonus of up to 5% of quarterly earnings, a $50 monthly tool allowance, a $50 monthly cell phone allowance, a company fleet vehicle, and a fuel card. OSHA training is provided. Corporate-level benefits are included.

The growth path is clear, too. Strong technicians here move into Lead Technician roles and eventually Field Maintenance Manager positions as Lessen continues expanding into new markets. If you want a maintenance job that's also a career track, the structure exists here to support that.

What separates strong candidates from average ones in this kind of role isn't just technical skill. It's the ability to document well, communicate clearly with a remote team, and close work orders accurately the first time. Lessen's platform is data-driven, which means your work order history and quality scores are visible. Techs who treat every ticket as a reflection of their professionalism tend to thrive. Techs who cut corners on documentation don't last long, regardless of how good their hands-on skills are.

job.title: Assistant General Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto's Assistant General Manager role in Annapolis sits squarely between the leasing floor and the GM's office. You're not purely a closer, and you're not purely an ops person. You do both, often on the same afternoon. That duality is the whole point of the job.

On the leasing side, you're conducting tours that actually convert. Not scripted walkthroughs, but conversations that help a prospective resident picture their life in the community. Traffic-to-lease ratios matter here. So does retention. You're working to make sure every resident interaction lands well enough that renewals feel like an easy yes rather than a negotiation.

On the financial side, you're in the rent collection process daily. Posting receipts, watching delinquency, keeping accounts receivable tight. You don't need to be an accountant, but you do need to understand why a delinquency spike at month-end is everyone's problem and how to address it before it becomes the GM's headache.

When the GM is out, you're running the show. That means coaching the team, keeping the property's interior and exterior presentation sharp, and solving resident issues without escalating everything upward. The ability to make a confident call without backup is something Bozzuto is specifically looking for here.

Weekend availability is part of the deal. One to two weekends per month, with weekday days off in exchange. That's when prospective residents are actually out looking, so it's when the leasing work gets done. It's not unusual in this business, but it's worth knowing upfront.

What separates strong AGM candidates from average ones at a company like Bozzuto isn't just sales ability. It's the combination of interpersonal range and operational discipline. Plenty of people can give a good tour. Fewer can give a great tour and then go back to the office and clean up a delinquency report without losing momentum. That combination is what this role actually demands.

What you bring

  • Four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a client-facing role with some exposure to rent collection, budgets, or accounts receivable
  • Comfort with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Active presence on social platforms and the ability to create content that reflects a community's character
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and the professional polish to represent the brand on-site

The salary range runs from roughly $72,600 to $75,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone looking to grow toward a General Manager seat, this role is a direct path. Bozzuto promotes from within, and the AGM position is exactly where that track starts.
